#!/bin/bash

# Define function to check password quality
check_password() {
  local password=$1
  local consecutive_chars=3

  # Check if password contains more than three consecutive identical characters
  if [[ $password =~ (.)\1{$consecutive_chars} ]]; then
    echo "Password rejected: contains more than $consecutive_chars consecutive identical characters" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  # Password is valid, return success
  return 0
}

# Define function to enforce password quality policy persistently for all password changes
enforce_password_quality() {
  local passwd_file=/etc/passwd
  local shadow_file=/etc/shadow

  # Check if password file exists and is readable
  if [[ ! -r $passwd_file ]]; then
    echo "Error: cannot read password file" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  # Check if shadow file exists and is readable
  if [[ ! -r $shadow_file ]]; then
    echo "Error: cannot read shadow file" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  # Loop through all users in password file
  while IFS=: read -r user _; do
    # Get current password hash for user
    local current_hash=$(grep "^$user:" $shadow_file | cut -d: -f2)

    # Check if current password hash is valid
    if [[ $current_hash =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9/$.]{13}$ ]]; then
      # Get new password from user input
      local new_password=$(dialog --inputbox "Enter new password for $user:" 8 70)

      # Check if new password is valid
      if check_password "$new_password"; then
        # Update password hash in shadow file
        sed -i "/^$user:/s/^\([^:]*:\)\{2\}\(.*\)/\1$(openssl passwd -1 $new_password)/" $shadow_file
      else
        echo "Error: invalid new password for $user" >&2
      fi
    fi
  done < $passwd_file
}

# Enforce password quality policy persistently for all password changes
enforce_password_quality

#!/bin/bash

# Get the current date in seconds since epoch
current_date=$(date +%s)

# Loop through all local user accounts
for user in $(cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd); do
  # Check if the account is a local user account
  if [ "$(getent passwd $user | cut -d: -f7)" = "/usr/sbin/nologin" ]; then
    # Get the password age for the current user
    password_age=$(chage --list $user | grep "Password expires" | awk '{print $3}')

    # Check if the password age is less than 1 day (24 hours)
    if [ "$password_age" -lt 86400 ]; then
      # Set the new password age to 1 day (24 hours)
      chage --mindays 1 $user
    fi
  fi
done
